import pandas as pd
from dateutil.parser import parse
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

OUTPUT_DIR = './output'

def task_func(csv_path=os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'data.csv'), date_column='date'):
    # Read the CSV file
    df = pd.read_csv(csv_path)

    # Convert the date column to datetime objects
    df[date_column] = df[date_column].apply(parse)

    # Extract the year from the datetime objects and create a Series for the year distribution
    years = df[date_column].dt.year
    year_distribution = pd.Series(years.value_counts())

    # Create a histogram of the year distribution
    fig, ax = plt.subplots()
    ax.bar(year_distribution.index, year_distribution.values)
    ax.set_xlabel('Year')
    ax.set_ylabel('Count')
    ax.set_title('Year Distribution of Dates')

    # Save the histogram plot and return the hist_plot and year_distribution
    plt.savefig(os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'year_histogram.png'))
    plt.close()
    return fig, year_distribution
